essence: Trains operators, machinists, and engineers in CNC machining, programming, safety, and best‑practice manufacturing techniques.

identity.title: CNC Technical Trainer
identity.summary: Delivers training on CNC operation, programming, tooling, safety, and process optimisation, helping individuals and teams develop advanced machining skills.

salary.notes: Trainers working for OEMs, aerospace firms, or national training centres can exceed £75k-£95k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, programming, or applications engineering
Strong understanding of tooling, feeds, speeds, and machining strategies
Ability to teach technical concepts clearly and effectively
Knowledge of safety standards, quality control, and manufacturing processes

lifestyle.description: CNC technical trainers work for manufacturers, training centres, OEMs, and engineering organisations. They deliver hands‑on and classroom training, develop learning materials, and help teams improve machining performance. The role suits individuals who enjoy teaching, communication, and sharing deep technical expertise.

habits: Delivers CNC training sessions and workshops
Teaches programming, tooling, and machining strategies
Assesses trainee performance and provides feedback
Keeps up‑to‑date with new CNC technologies and methods
